# Splice

> Browser cognition infrastructure for autonomous agents: an MCP server that gives coding
> agents a self-healing Chromium with verified actions, delta observations, failure
> forensics, recovery memory, prompt optimization, accessibility audits, and behavior
> reports for recursive self-improvement.

## Install (for agents)

- [Agent Install Guide](AGENT_INSTALL.md): deterministic install + verification steps. Summary: `npm install splice`, `npx splice init`, register with your MCP client (`claude mcp add splice -- npx -y splice start`), then verify with `npx splice doctor --json` until `"healthy": true`.

## Docs

- [README](README.md): full feature and tool reference.
- MCP resource `splice://guide/agent-playbook` (after install): the recommended cognition loop — diagnose → compile verified action → verify → recover.

## Key facts

- MCP server command: `splice start` (stdio). Requires Node >= 20 and Playwright Chromium.
- Recommended loop: navigate → diagnose_agent_state → compile_verified_action (with declared `expect` postconditions) → verify via deltas.
- Long runs: call generate_behavior_report periodically and apply its selfImprovement section.
- Prompts: run optimize_prompt on conversational intents; enable promptOptimization in splice.config.json for automatic rewrites (always reported, never silent).
- Introspection stack (builds itself, zero extra calls): every decision is mapped in J-space (get_jspace_map), screened for geometry hazards that attach to plan evidence (get_jspace_detections), calibrated against verified outcomes, explainable in plain language (explain_last_decision), testable across phrasings before acting (run_intent_experiment), and reconstructed into a train-of-thought report with a ranked chain-of-thinking optimization plan (generate_thought_report).
- Session artifacts for the next run: .splice/jspace/ and .splice/thought/ are written automatically at session close; read them at startup (`splice report --jspace` / `--thought` offline).
